name: AccessibilityService.takeScreenshot
version: API 30 for display capture; API 34 for window capture
capture_driver: Accessibility service triggers native display or window screenshots from the system
compose_support: Indirect; captures rendered Compose UI as part of the screen or window
view_support: Display and individual windows; works for any visible app UI, including WebView and RecyclerView
stitching_strategy: No built-in long-screenshot stitcher; long capture requires repeated captures or scroll-capture integration
confidence_fallback: Use onFailure() error codes and retry after the 333 ms minimum interval
static_chrome_handling: Captures the requested display or window; takeScreenshotOfWindow omits overlay windows, but long-screen capture still depends on app content and system scroll support
dynamic_content_robustness: Single-frame captures can miss changing content; repeated captures need app-side timing and stitching
content_type_fitness: Good for static screens and single windows; long pages need extra capture logic
memory_strategy: HardwareBuffer-backed screenshot result, then bitmap conversion by the caller
stitching_speed: Single-shot; repeated capture speed is bounded by the 333 ms request interval
max_output_constraints: Bounded by display or window size and available buffer memory
oem_compatibility: Depends on vendor screenshot behavior and accessibility service policy
windowing_behavior: Can target a display or a specific window; window capture excludes outside overlays
permissions_required: Accessibility service permission plus canTakeScreenshot capability
chart_stitching_fitness: Moderate for static charts, but long-chart stitching is app-side
chat_stitching_fitness: Moderate to good for static chat screens; long threads need app-side stitching
